mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces a negative 20-day return in more than 50% of observations when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity cash flow projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side disruption and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO falls below the -0.5% threshold.
